jobs.title: Program Coordinator
jobs.description:
At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at jnj.com.

As guided by Our Credo, Johnson & Johnson is responsible to our employees who work with us throughout the world. We provide an inclusive work environment where each person is considered as an individual. At Johnson & Johnson, we respect the diversity and dignity of our employees and recognize their merit.

Job Function

Project/Program Management Group

Job Sub Function

Project/Program Management

Job Category

Professional

All Job Posting Locations:

Bangalore, Karnataka, India

Job Description

Role: Program Coordinator (PMO)

Duration:
12 Months (Fixed-Term)

Location:
Bangalore

Role Overview

The Program Coordinator (PMO) will support the execution of a large-scale global HR Digital Transformation program. This role will help drive structured program delivery, ensure transparency of progress, and enable effective coordination across multiple workstreams.

This position is ideal for a junior to mid-level professional who can operate with guidance, ensuring governance processes run efficiently, program data remains accurate, and teams stay aligned on priorities, breakthroughs, and deliverables.

Key Responsibilities

Program Coordination & Support

Partner with Program and Project Managers in coordinating day-to-day program activities across multiple workstreams with global stakeholders
Track progress against key program achievements and highlight variances or gaps
Track and manage program interdependencies across workstreams

Governance & Reporting Support

Support Weekly Program Reporting & Governance Cycles By

Orchestrating updates from workstream leads & project managers
Ensuring that inputs are put into standard templates for weekly program meetings
Preparing draft program status reports and dashboards
Maintain and update overall program plan and milestone trackers to reflect current delivery status
Ensure consistency and accuracy of reporting data

RAID Tracking & Follow-Up

Own and maintain the program level RAID (Risks, Actions, Issues, Dependencies) log in SharePoint including conducting regular audits
Follow up with owners to ensure updates are current and actions are progressing
Highlight overdue or high-risk items to the Program Manager / Director
Support structured escalation where needed

Meeting Coordination

Schedule and coordinate recurring program level meetings and ad-hoc workstream meetings
Prepare meeting agendas in advance and working files and capture clear meeting notes, decisions, and action items
Maintain action trackers and follow up to ensure timely closure
Support smooth communication across globally distributed teams

Documentation & Administration

Maintain Program Documentation, Including

Status reports and governance artifacts
Meeting records and decision logs
Ensure all materials are stored in the correct locations with proper version control
Support general PMO administrative activities (e.g., templates, trackers, access coordination)

Team Support & Onboarding

Support onboarding of new team members (access, materials, orientation)
Assist with coordinating tools, access, and documentation
Provide general operational support to enable efficient program execution

Qualifications

Education

Project management certifications or equivalent experience in Business Administration, Project Management, Information Technology, Human Resources, or a related field
Equivalent combination of education and relevant project coordination or PMO experience may also be considered
PMO, project management, or governance-related certification is an advantage
Preferred to be J&J FPX Certified

Experience

Experience and Skills Required:

2–5 years of experience in a project coordination, PMO, or program support role
Experience working in structured project environments with formal reporting and tracking
Exposure to multi-stakeholder or cross-functional programs is helpful

Skills

Strong interpersonal and coordination skills
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint) and collaboration tools (e.g., SharePoint)
Good written and verbal communication skills
Strong ability to manage structured reporting, trackers, and documentation with high accuracy and deliver to deadlines
Strong follow-up discipline and attention to detail

Preferred

Experience in PMO or governance support roles
Familiarity with RAID tracking and structured reporting processes
Exposure to digital transformation, HR, or enterprise programs

Johnson & Johnson is an Affirmative Action and Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, or protected veteran status and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability.

Required Skills

Preferred Skills:

Agility Jumps, Analytical Reasoning, Business Behavior, Collaboration Tools, Cost-Benefit Analysis (CBA), Data Analysis, Execution Focus, Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL), Process Oriented, Project Management Methodology (PMM), Project Management Office (PMO), Project Reporting, Project Support, Stakeholder Engagement, Statement of Work (SOW), Time Management

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Other

Industries

Hospitals and Health Care
